name: Tostada Casserole
recipeInstructions:
1. Coarsely break taco shells. In a 2-quart bowl, mix ground beef and onion; cover bowl with waxed paper. Cook at high heat for 4-5 minutes, stirring and breaking up meat into small pieces every 2 minutes. Tilt the bowl and discard excess fat. Stir in tomato sauce, water, and chili powder. Cover and cook at high heat for another 4 minutes or until the meat mixture thickens, stirring after 2 minutes. Stir in undrained green chilies and shredded cheese.
2. In a shallow 1-1/2-quart casserole dish, place three-quarters of the coarsely broken tacos. Top with the meat mixture; cover with the casserole lid or large plate. Cook at medium-high heat for 12 to 14 minutes until the meat mixture is hot. Let stand, still covered, for 5 minutes. Sprinkle with remaining tacos.
